title: Read-only audit in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public. Focus on constraints: source naming, bound/unbound Project OS envelope rules, and whether Claude/FUGUE sources would require extra fields or fail validation for GHA backup recording. Return actionable conclusion with file evidence.
summary_text: Process note: this is a single-lane vote; per Kernel orchestration policy, treat single-lane fallback as a workflow violation and request parallel corroboration before merge. **Preferred Design** 1. Keep current defaults, add explicit override surface at wrapper level. - Current hardcoded defaults are in [kernel-run-complete.sh:114](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh:114) and [kernel-milestone-record.sh:144](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-milestone-record.sh:144). - Add `assistant/source` override inputs (env + optional flags) there, but default to existing values (`codex`, `kernel-*`). 2. Add Project OS passthrough in the backup runner. - Extend [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:166](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:166) options with: `--project-os-ticket-id`, `--acceptance`, `--authority-scope`. - Pass them through to `python3 -m codex_kernel_guard.cli backup-record` near [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:274](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:274). 3. Add fail-fast guard for bound sources when external validator is active. - `codex_kernel_guard` treats `source.startswith("kernel")` as bound and requires ticket/acceptance/scope ([task_backup.py:263](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:263), [task_backup.py:274](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:274)). - Guard in wrapper should error clearly if kernel source is used without required Project OS fields, instead of silent downgrade. **Rejected Alternative** - Auto-rewrite kernel sources to non-kernel labels (or auto-pick “latest” ticket) when fields are missing. - Reason: weakens audit semantics and can record unverifiable provenance while appearing green. **Test Expectations** 1. Defaults unchanged. - Existing tests that assert `--source kernel-run-complete|kernel-phase-complete|kernel-progress-save` should still pass. 2. Override path. - Add tests that stub runner and verify overridden `assistant/source` are forwarded from `kernel-run-complete` and `kernel-milestone-record`. 3. Project OS passthrough. - Add tests that verify new Project OS flags are forwarded by `run-kernel-task-completion-backup`. 4. Bound/unbound behavior. - Kernel source + missing Project OS fields => clear non-zero failure when external tool exists. - Non-kernel source (e.g., `claude-stop-hook`/`fugue-stop-hook`) => succeeds without Project OS fields.
